features.description: Fix lyrics auto-scroll hijacking the page. Currently in SyncedLyrics.tsx, scrollIntoView fires on every active line change, which yanks the page away from wherever the user scrolled to (e.g., reading the backstory). Add a toggle button above the lyrics section labeled 'Follow lyrics' (on by default). When ON, lyrics auto-scroll to the active line. When OFF, no auto-scrolling happens and the user can freely scroll. Store the toggle state so it persists during the session. DONE: a visible toggle button exists above lyrics, clicking it disables auto-scroll, user can read backstory without the page jumping. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback, lyrics content, track data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, play a song, scroll to backstory, confirm page does not jump when toggle is off.
steps: Add a follow/unfollow toggle state to SyncedLyrics.tsx
Only call scrollIntoView when toggle is ON
Render a small toggle button above the lyrics section
pnpm build passes

features.description: Fix album looping instead of advancing to next album. The auto-play next album logic exists in audio-context.tsx but users report the album loops back to track 1 instead of moving to the next album. Debug the onended handler: check if the queue is being reset to the same album's tracks instead of the next album. The code at the 'Queue exhausted' comment should advance to the next album, but something is preventing it. DONE: when the last track of Founders finishes, Product album starts. When last album (The Human Side) finishes, playback stops. DO NOT TOUCH: audio files, lyrics, UI components. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, play the last track of any album and confirm next album starts automatically.
steps: Read audio-context.tsx and trace the onended flow
Find why album loops instead of advancing
Fix the queue logic so next album plays
Test: last track of album -> next album starts
pnpm build passes

features.description: Ensure audio plays continuously across page navigation. When a user clicks into a track page while a song is playing, the audio should keep playing without interruption. The audio context uses React context which should persist across client-side navigation, but users report the song stops or has trouble playing when navigating to a track page from the album page. Debug and fix. DONE: click a track on album page while another song plays, navigate to track detail page, audio never stops or glitches. DO NOT TOUCH: lyrics, track data, album structure. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, start playing a song on album page, click into a different track's detail page, confirm audio keeps playing.
steps: Read audio-context.tsx provider setup
Check if AudioProvider wraps the app in layout.tsx
Ensure audio element is not destroyed on route change
Fix any issues found
pnpm build passes

features.description: Show verse/chorus/bridge section labels in lyrics display. The lyrics in lib/albums.ts already have structure tags like [Verse 1], [Chorus], [Bridge], [Outro] embedded in the lyric text. These should render as visible section headers in the lyrics display, styled differently from lyric lines (e.g., smaller, muted, uppercase). Currently SyncedLyrics.tsx may be hiding or not styling these. DONE: section labels like 'Verse 1', 'Chorus', 'Bridge' appear as styled headers between lyric lines. DO NOT TOUCH: audio files, the actual lyric text content, track data structure. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, open any track page and see section labels displayed in the lyrics.
steps: Check how SyncedLyrics.tsx handles [Verse 1] etc tags
Render them as styled section headers (small, muted, uppercase)
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add 'Up Next' countdown when a song ends. When the current track finishes and there is a next track in the queue, show a countdown overlay or banner: 'Up next: [Song Title] — playing in 5...' counting down from 5 to 1. Include a 'Cancel' button that stops the countdown and pauses playback. If countdown reaches 0, the next track plays. Show this on the track page (TrackPageClient.tsx) and optionally in the NowPlayingBar. DONE: countdown appears when song ends, shows next track title, counts down 5 seconds, Cancel button works. DO NOT TOUCH: audio files, lyrics, album data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, play a song to the end, see countdown with cancel button, confirm next song plays after 5 seconds or stops on cancel.
steps: Add a countdown state to audio-context or TrackPageClient
When track ends and next exists, start 5-second countdown
Show countdown UI with next track name and Cancel button
Cancel stops countdown and pauses, countdown end plays next
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add cover art thumbnail to the NowPlayingBar. The NowPlayingBar currently shows track title and album name but no cover image. Add a small cover art thumbnail (~36x36 or ~40x40, rounded) to the left of the track info in the NowPlayingBar. Use the track's coverImage field. DONE: NowPlayingBar shows small album art next to track title. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback logic, lyrics, track data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, play any track and see cover art in the bottom bar.
steps: Import Image from next/image in NowPlayingBar.tsx
Add cover art thumbnail to the left of track title
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add genre/mood tags to tracks. Add a 'tags' field to each track in lib/albums.ts with relevant descriptive tags (e.g., vibe-coding gets ['satire', 'comedy'], ship-it gets ['motivation', 'velocity'], imposter gets ['vulnerability', 'real talk']). Display tags on the track page as small pill badges below the genre/mood line. Also show tags on album page track rows. Choose 1-3 tags per song that capture the tone/vibe. DONE: each track has tags, visible as pills on track page and album page. DO NOT TOUCH: audio files, lyrics content. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, open vibe-coding track page and see 'satire' tag.
steps: Add tags: string[] to Track type in lib/types.ts
Add tags to each track in lib/albums.ts (1-3 per song)
Display as pill badges on TrackPageClient.tsx
Optionally show on album page track rows
pnpm build passes

features.description: Fix broken source links. Many 'Inspired by' source links on track pages are still not working or point to wrong URLs. Re-verify ALL source URLs in lib/albums.ts by: (1) calling mcp__lennysdata__search_content for each source's guest name, (2) finding the exact matching Lenny Substack article, (3) calling mcp__lennysdata__read_content to confirm. Specifically fix vibe-coding sources to link to Lenny's podcast episode with Lazar Jovanovic (the Lovable vibe coder). For any source where no Lenny article exists, link to the original external source and update the title to indicate it (e.g., 'paulgraham.com'). DONE: every source link that has a URL opens a real, working page. DO NOT TOUCH: audio files, lyrics, UI components. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, click every source link on every track page and confirm they all load real pages.
steps: Read all source URLs from lib/albums.ts
Test each URL - identify broken ones
Re-research broken URLs using Lenny MCP tools
Update lib/albums.ts with correct URLs
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add a subtle affordance to NowPlayingBar so users know they can click to view the playing song's detail page. Add a small clickable area or visual cue (e.g., the track title is a link, or a small chevron/arrow icon, or the entire left section of the bar is clickable and navigates to /track/{slug}). Make it subtle but discoverable. DONE: clicking the track title/info area in NowPlayingBar navigates to that track's detail page. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback, lyrics, track data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, click the track title in the NowPlayingBar and confirm it navigates to the track page without stopping playback.
steps: Wrap track title/info in NowPlayingBar with a Link to /track/{slug}
Add subtle hover style to indicate clickability
pnpm build passes

features.description: Navigate to the track page when a new song starts playing. When the audio context switches to a new track (from auto-play, queue, or 'up next' countdown), automatically navigate the browser to /track/{new-track-slug} so the user sees the lyrics and details for the song they're hearing. Use Next.js router.push(). Only navigate if the user is NOT already on that track's page. DONE: when a new song starts (from any source), the page navigates to that track's detail page. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback continuity, lyrics, track data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, let a song end and auto-play next, confirm page navigates to the new track page.
steps: In audio-context.tsx or a wrapper, detect when currentTrack changes
Call router.push to navigate to the new track page
Skip if already on that track page
pnpm build passes

features.description: Make the progress/seek bar thicker and easier to click. The time progress bar on the track page (TrackPageClient.tsx) and in the NowPlayingBar is too thin and hard to click/tap, especially on mobile. Increase the bar height to at least 6-8px (from current ~2-4px). Make the hover state even thicker (~10-12px) so it's easy to grab. Also ensure the clickable/draggable area extends beyond the visual bar (add padding to the hit target). DONE: progress bar is visually thicker and easy to click on both desktop and mobile. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback logic, lyrics, track data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, open track page and confirm progress bar is thicker and easy to click.
steps: Find progress bar styling in TrackPageClient.tsx and NowPlayingBar.tsx
Increase bar height to 6-8px default, 10-12px on hover
Add padding to increase click target area
pnpm build passes

features.description: Fix next/previous track buttons to navigate across albums. Currently clicking next track on the last song of an album does not advance to the first song of the next album. The next button should work across album boundaries: last track of Founders -> first track of Product, etc. Same for previous: first track of Product -> last track of Founders. When on the very last track of the last album, next should be disabled. When on the very first track of the first album, previous should be disabled. DONE: next/prev buttons work across album boundaries. DO NOT TOUCH: audio files, lyrics, UI styling. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, play last track of Founders, click next, confirm Product album first track plays.
steps: Read audio-context.tsx next/previous track logic
Update to handle cross-album navigation
Disable buttons at absolute start/end
pnpm build passes

features.description: Add email signup form to the homepage. Create a Supabase client utility at lib/supabase.ts that reads NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from environment. Add an email capture section near the bottom of the homepage (app/page.tsx) with a heading like 'More songs coming — get notified', a single email input field, and a submit button. On submit, insert the email into the 'email_subscribers' table in Supabase (columns: id uuid auto, email text unique, created_at timestamptz auto). Show a success message after insert. Handle duplicate emails gracefully (show 'You're already subscribed!' instead of an error). Style it to match the existing site aesthetic — warm Lenny theme, not dark Spotify. Keep it simple: no auth, no confirmation email, just capture the email. DONE: email input on homepage, submitting stores email in Supabase, success/duplicate messages work. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback, lyrics, track data, album pages. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, enter an email on homepage, check Supabase table has the row, enter same email again and see 'already subscribed' message.
steps: Create lib/supabase.ts with createClient using env vars
Add EmailSignup client component in components/EmailSignup.tsx
Add EmailSignup to homepage app/page.tsx near bottom
Handle submit: insert into email_subscribers, show success
Handle duplicate: catch unique constraint, show already subscribed
pnpm build passes

features.description: Fix progress/seek bar: clickable area must match visible bar thickness, and fill must animate smoothly. Currently the bar was made thicker visually but the actual clickable/draggable hit target is still the old thin size, so users miss clicks. Also the progress fill updates in jerky steps instead of smooth animation. Fix both issues in TrackPageClient.tsx and NowPlayingBar.tsx: (1) make the clickable hit target at least as wide/tall as the visible bar by ensuring the input range or click handler div covers the full visual area, (2) use CSS transition on the fill width (e.g., transition: width 0.3s linear) so it fills smoothly between updates instead of jumping. DONE: clicking anywhere on the visible bar works to seek, and the fill animates smoothly during playback. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback logic, lyrics, track data. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, click various spots on the progress bar and confirm they all register, watch the fill move smoothly during playback.
steps: Read current progress bar implementation in TrackPageClient.tsx and NowPlayingBar.tsx
Ensure clickable area matches or exceeds visible bar size
Add CSS transition to progress fill for smooth animation
pnpm build passes

features.description: Remove the 'Follow lyrics' toggle and all auto-scroll behavior from the lyrics display. The current implementation is buggy and distracting — it either scrolls when you don't want it to or doesn't work properly. Remove the toggle button entirely from SyncedLyrics.tsx. Remove all scrollIntoView calls related to lyrics auto-scrolling. The lyrics should simply be static text that the user scrolls manually. Keep the active line highlighting (current line styled differently) but do NOT auto-scroll to it. DONE: no toggle button visible, no auto-scrolling happens at any time, active line is still highlighted, user scrolls lyrics manually. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback, track data, lyrics content. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, play a song, confirm no auto-scrolling happens and no toggle button exists, confirm active line is still highlighted.
steps: Remove follow/unfollow toggle state and UI from SyncedLyrics.tsx
Remove all scrollIntoView calls for lyrics
Keep active line highlighting CSS
pnpm build passes

features.description: Fix SEO, Open Graph image, and favicon. The current link preview when sharing on LinkedIn/Twitter/iMessage is ugly — it shows a generic Next.js placeholder. Fix this: (1) Create an OG image using Next.js built-in opengraph-image route (app/opengraph-image.tsx) that generates a 1200x630 image with the site title 'Lenny's Greatest Hits', subtitle 'Lenny Rachitsky's best insights turned into actual songs', and a warm gradient background matching the site's color scheme. Use ImageResponse from next/og. (2) Replace the default favicon.ico in app/ with a music-note emoji favicon generated via an app/icon.tsx route using ImageResponse — render a musical note emoji on a warm-toned circle background. (3) Update metadata in app/layout.tsx to include the OG image reference and ensure all meta tags are complete (og:type as 'website', og:site_name, twitter:card as 'summary_large_image'). (4) Add structured data (JSON-LD) in layout.tsx for MusicAlbum or WebApplication schema. DONE: sharing the URL on any platform shows a polished 1200x630 preview image with the site name, and the browser tab shows a music-note favicon instead of the default Next.js icon. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback, lyrics, track data, page layouts. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, check /opengraph-image returns a 1200x630 image, check /icon returns a favicon, inspect page source for complete meta tags.
steps: Create app/opengraph-image.tsx using ImageResponse with warm gradient + title
Create app/icon.tsx using ImageResponse with music note emoji
Delete old app/favicon.ico
Update metadata in app/layout.tsx with og:type, og:site_name, twitter:card
Add JSON-LD structured data in layout.tsx
pnpm build passes

features.description: Remove all fade-up / fade-in / slide-in text animations from the entire app. These are vibe-cody AI-generated animation patterns that look cheap and unpolished. Search all .tsx files for any framer-motion 'motion.' components that animate opacity, translateY, y, or similar entrance animations on text, headings, paragraphs, or cards. Remove the motion wrapper and render the element as a plain HTML element instead. Keep framer-motion ONLY for functional animations like the audio visualizer or progress bar — remove it from all decorative text entrance effects. DONE: no text fades in or slides up when loading any page. All text appears instantly. DO NOT TOUCH: audio playback, lyrics content, track data, audio visualizer animations. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, load homepage and album pages and confirm all text appears immediately with no fade/slide animations.
steps: Search all .tsx files for motion.div, motion.h1, motion.p, motion.span etc with opacity/y animations
Replace motion elements with plain HTML equivalents
Remove unused framer-motion imports if no motion elements remain in file
pnpm build passes
